_comment: Source: Michigan MDHHS Section 7-4 Vulnerable Adult Abuse (Revised 1/5/2023, MDHHS Reviewed 2023). Base Michigan template — MCA-specific selections not configured.
protocol_reference: Michigan Procedures Protocol Section 7-4: Vulnerable Adult Abuse — MDHHS Approved 1/5/2023; MCL 400.11a
purpose: EMS providers are mandatory reporters of suspected vulnerable adult abuse, neglect, or exploitation under MCL 400.11a. A vulnerable adult is a person ≥ 18 years old who, because of age, developmental disability, mental illness, or physical limitation, is unable to protect themselves. Reporting to a supervisor does NOT fulfill the individual's mandatory reporting obligation.

sections.title: Definition of Vulnerable Adult
points: Person ≥ 18 years old who is unable to protect themselves from abuse, neglect, or exploitation due to:
— Age (typically elderly)
— Developmental disability
— Mental illness
— Physical limitation or impairment
Abuse: intentional physical, mental, or emotional injury or assault
Neglect: failure to provide adequate food, clothing, shelter, or medical care
Exploitation: misuse of funds, property, or personal dignity — includes undue influence, financial coercion, theft, or misappropriation of assets

sections.title: Indicators of Possible Vulnerable Adult Abuse
points: PHYSICAL indicators: injury inconsistent with stated mechanism or functional status; staged burns; possible over-sedation or over-medication; malnourishment or dehydration without a medical cause; unexplained bruising, wounds, or fractures

ENVIRONMENTAL indicators: confined to a single room or small space without adequate food, water, or medical care; hoarding that creates hazardous conditions; presence of squalor; lack of heat, electricity, or running water; caregiver controls access

PSYCHOSOCIAL indicators: patient disappears from regular contact with family or friends; inappropriate fear of caregiver or family member; caregiver speaks for patient, prevents private conversation, appears indifferent or hostile
EXPLOITATION indicators: finances surrendered to another person; unexplained or sudden asset transfers; recent changes to will or power of attorney; caregiver receives financial benefit from patient

sections.title: Reporting Obligations
points: ALL EMS personnel are mandatory reporters under MCL 400.11a
Call 855-444-3911 IMMEDIATELY to report suspected abuse, neglect, or exploitation
Do NOT rely on others (supervisor, colleague, nurse) to make the report — each individual must report directly
Reporting to the EMS organization head does NOT fulfill the mandatory reporting requirement
Report is required even if the patient refuses transport
Do NOT accuse or confront the suspected abuser at the scene
Document objective findings, verbatim statements, and environmental observations in the PCR

sections.title: Scene and Documentation Guidance
sections.reference: Michigan Protocol 7-4: Vulnerable Adult — Scene and Documentation
points: Primary duty is emergency medical care — treat and stabilize per appropriate protocol
Document verbatim any spontaneous statements made by the patient
Document the patient's demeanor, affect, and level of fear
Document all injuries with precise descriptions (location, size, color, pattern, estimated age)
Document environmental conditions relevant to neglect
Document caregivers present and their relationship to the patient
Mandatory report is required even when the patient is not transported

deterioration_flags: Life-threatening injuries — stabilize and transport per appropriate treatment protocol; mandatory report still required
Patient in immediate danger — involve law enforcement for scene safety while maintaining patient care
Caregiver interfering with care — involve law enforcement; prioritize patient care and safety
scope_notes: ALL EMS provider levels are mandatory reporters under MCL 400.11a
Mandatory report hotline: 855-444-3911 — call immediately upon suspicion
Reporting to supervisor alone does NOT fulfill legal obligation
Report is required even when patient is not transported
Do not confront suspected abusers — that is a law enforcement function
